etymology.description: 声(聲) (shēng, 'sound') + 殳(殳) (shū, 'hand holding a hammer or a mallet') = a stone chime being struck by a mallet
etymology.interpretation: The character 殸 depicts the action of striking a stone chime with a mallet, illustrating the sound produced by this action. The components relate directly to the sound of a stone chime, emphasizing the sonic aspect emphasized by 殸.

parts.english: hand holding a hammer or a mallet
